untranslatable_reason: This is a multi-asset factor model strategy using principal component analysis on mean-centered historical returns to compute expected returns and rank stocks. The backtest engine supports single-asset technical indicator rules only. The signal requires: (1) cross-sectional PCA decomposition across all stocks, (2) eigenvalue/eigenvector computation, (3) OLS regression for factor return estimation, and (4) forward-looking expected return calculation. None of these quantitative factor model operations can be expressed using the available single-asset technical indicators (RSI, moving averages, Bollinger Bands, etc.) or the allowed operators (>, <, crosses_above, etc.). This requires a dedicated factor model backtesting engine with portfolio construction capabilities, not a single-asset rule-based engine.
translation_notes: Signal CHAN_QT_DRY_0 is a sophisticated cross-sectional factor model strategy that fundamentally operates at a different level than the rule-based technical indicator framework. To implement this signal, you would need: (1) a matrix algebra module, (2) covariance matrix computation, (3) eigendecomposition, (4) OLS regression capabilities, (5) cross-sectional ranking and portfolio construction logic. The current backtest engine cannot support this architecture.
